claim: Marimo reactive notebooks enable rapid prototyping with automatic state-driven UI updates; reactive cells update when dependencies change, unlike static Jupyter notebooks
actionable: Use marimo for prompt experimentation and testing; set up reactive text areas and dropdowns to dynamically test the same prompt against multiple LLM models with one click
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Prompt libraries with variable templates (content slots defined with delimiters) enable reuse; marimo can parse variables and auto-generate UI for user input
actionable: Structure your prompts with consistent variable delimiters (e.g., {{content}}); build marimo apps that detect these patterns and generate text areas automatically
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Interactive prompt notebooks should support multi-model execution with one checkbox; compare outputs from o1-preview, o1-mini, Claude, and Gemini simultaneously for insight
actionable: Build a prompt testing harness in marimo that maintains a model list; add a 'run on all' checkbox to batch-test the same prompt across models
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Marimo's command-dot mode-switching (user mode ↔ code mode) enables two audiences: product consumers see clean UI, engineers see implementation instantly
actionable: Use marimo for sharing prompts with non-technical stakeholders; toggle between clean UI and code to explain how something works without overwhelming them
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Prompt management is critical knowledge work; reusing and versioning prompts at scale beats writing one-off prompts; treat prompts like first-class code artifacts
actionable: Version control and document your prompts; build a living prompt library that you incrementally improve over time, just like source code
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Large language models generate output based on temperature settings; for o1 and Gemini reasoning models, temperature is constrained to 1.0 to prevent randomness in reasoning
actionable: When testing reasoning models, lock temperature to 1.0; for other models, use lower temperatures (0.3-0.7) for consistency, higher (0.8-1.0) for diversity
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Marimo notebooks are 'just Python' with cells as decorated functions; this enables easy version control, AI-assisted editing (with Cursor), and integration with existing tooling
actionable: Use marimo over Jupyter for AI-assisted development; git-friendly cell design enables cursor/Claude to help you write and modify notebooks
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini

claim: Prompt engineering was initially a meme but is now a serious profession; engineers have an advantage because they can build tools like marimo to manipulate prompts faster than others
actionable: Invest heavily in prompt engineering infrastructure and tools; this is your competitive advantage as an engineer in the age of LLMs
source_title: Engineer your Prompt Library: Marimo Notebooks with o1-mini, Claude, Gemini
